response: Imagine you and all your playground friends each draw a sticky note with who shared their cookie that day, then stick all your notes in a chain that every single one of you carries an identical copy of. No one can scribble out or change a sticky note secretly, because every friend has the exact same chain to check and make sure everyone’s being honest. That’s a tiny, fun version of blockchain: a super big, shared list that everyone can check but no one can sneakily alter.
